assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend has already been recognised as a receivable and gives rise to foreign currency exposure.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes hedge accounting for recognised assets or liabilities, and a recognised intragroup receivable raises the question whether it could be designated as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats forecast transactions as possible hedged items, and the context specifically addresses when intragroup foreign currency exposures may qualify in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The provided context includes IFRIC 16 and IFRS 9 guidance on hedges of foreign currency risk arising from net investments in foreign operations, which is a distinct hedge accounting model relevant to intragroup foreign currency exposures at consolidation level.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting under IAS 21
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 separately governs recognition of exchange differences on foreign currency monetary items and intragroup monetary balances in consolidated financial statements outside hedge accounting.
